nameMeanings.name: Langley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Langley or Longley (in Kent, Sussex, Hertfordshire, Norfolk, Somerset, Shrops, Lancashire, Durham, and many other counties), themselves from Old English lang ‘long’ + lēah ‘wood, glade, clearing’.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Langlif (Old Norse Langlíf ‘long’ + ‘life’ or ‘body’).

variants.name: Langlais
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Pierre, Emile, Marcel, Normand, Albertine, Armand, Cecile, Enoil, Herve, Jacques, Laurent.
French: ethnic name for an Englishman, from Anglais ‘Englishman’, with fused definite article l'. Compare Langley .

History: Daniel Sargent dit Sérien and Langlais from Worcester, MA married Marguerite Lavoie in Rivière-Ouelle, QC, in 1718. Joseph Weber dit Langlais from Boston, MA married Marie-Charlotte Guillet in Cap Saint-Ignace, QC, in 1725.

variants.name: Longley
meanings: English (mainly Nottinghamshire and South Yorkshire): variant of   Langley  . Compare   Longpre   2.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Langley was born about 1159, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Robert Meynell IV in 1186, in Derbyshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnes Langley est née environ 1159, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Robert Meynell IV en 1186, à Derbyshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Agnes Langley wurde um 1159 in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1186 in Derbyshire, England Robert Meynell IV geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Agnes Langley è nata il circa 1159 nel Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Robert Meynell IV nel 1186, nel Derbyshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Langleyは約1159年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアで生まれた。1186年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアでRobert Meynell IVと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Langley(은)는 대략 1159년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1186년 영국 더비실르에서 Robert Meynell IV(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다.
